name: Brazil
avoid: Carnival, New Year, and July school-holiday weeks push up flights and hotel prices fast.
Rio and Paraty are hottest and wettest from December to March, while Salvador sees its rainiest spell from April to July.
A May–October nationwide circuit is great for sightseeing, but Florianópolis is quieter and less beach-focused outside peak summer.

cities.name: Rio de Janeiro
cities.notes: Christ the Redeemer, Sugarloaf, samba nights, and beach-to-mountain scenery in one compact city.

cities.name: São Paulo
cities.notes: Brazil’s biggest food and culture capital with Paulista museums, markets, and design-heavy neighbourhoods.

cities.name: Iguaçu Falls
cities.notes: Brazil’s panoramic side of the falls pairs huge viewpoints with bird park and Itaipu add-ons.

cities.name: Salvador
cities.notes: Afro-Brazilian music, colonial Pelourinho lanes, and bay sunsets with strong Bahian food culture.

cities.name: Florianópolis
cities.notes: Island city mixing surf beaches, seafood villages, dunes, and a mellow south-Brazil pace.

cities.name: Paraty
cities.notes: Car-free colonial centre, cachaça distilleries, and boat days through emerald islands on the Costa Verde.

itinerary.sim: Claro or Vivo prepaid SIM/eSIM is the easiest option at GIG or GRU — around ₹680–₹1,020 typically gets 10–15GB for maps, ride-hailing, and hotspot backup. Keep offline maps downloaded for Costa Verde road stretches and around Iguaçu National Park.

Rio de Janeiro.name: Rio de Janeiro
Rio de Janeiro.note: Stay in Copacabana, Ipanema, or Flamengo, pre-book Christ and Sugarloaf timed entries, and lean on Uber after dark instead of wandering between nightlife districts.

activities.name: Trem do Corcovado return ticket for Christ the Redeemer
Parque das Ruínas viewpoints and Santa Teresa lanes
Escadaria Selarón and Arcos da Lapa walk
Santa Teresa tram ride if operating
Copacabana beach walk and Forte de Copacabana
Pão de Açúcar (Sugarloaf) cable car
Ipanema and Arpoador sunset circuit
Optional Museum of Tomorrow stop in Porto Maravilha
Museu do Amanhã (Museum of Tomorrow)
Real Gabinete Português de Leitura photo stop
Maracanã stadium tour
Evening samba club or roda in Lapa

São Paulo.name: São Paulo
São Paulo.note: Base yourself around Paulista or Jardins for quick metro access; Sunday mornings are great for Avenida Paulista car-free time, while museums are busiest on rainy weekends.

activities.name: MASP collection and Lina Bo Bardi building
Japan House São Paulo and Paulista cultural strip
Ibirapuera Park and Oscar Niemeyer landmarks
Optional rooftop drinks around Jardins
Mercado Municipal de São Paulo for mortadella sandwich and fruit stalls
Pinacoteca de São Paulo
Museu do Futebol at Pacaembu
Beco do Batman murals and Vila Madalena bars

Iguaçu Falls.name: Iguaçu Falls
Iguaçu Falls.note: Sleep in Foz do Iguaçu, start the national park early before tour buses arrive, and keep a dry bag ready if you add Macuco Safari or any catwalk viewpoint after rain.

activities.name: Parque Nacional do Iguaçu foreign visitor entry
Main panoramic walkway to the falls spray deck
Macuco Safari boat ride
Parque das Aves bird park
Itaipu Binacional panoramic visit
Marco das Três Fronteiras cultural complex
Dreams Ice Bar or Wax Museum if travelling with kids
Sunset over the Paraná River back in town

Salvador.name: Salvador
Salvador.note: Stay near Pelourinho or Barra for short transfers, dress lightly for the humidity, and plan Pelourinho walks by day or with a guided evening food/music circuit.

activities.name: Pelourinho lanes, Largo do Pelourinho, and live percussion corners
São Francisco Church and Convent
Casa do Carnaval da Bahia
Elevador Lacerda and Mercado Modelo
Farol da Barra and Nautical Museum of Bahia
MAM Bahia at Solar do Unhão
Sunset boat on Baía de Todos-os-Santos
Optional Praia do Forte full-day trip

Florianópolis.name: Florianópolis
Florianópolis.note: Without a car, split time between the central bridge area and one beach cluster; ride-hailing is easy, but beach-to-beach transfers take longer than they look on the map.

activities.name: Mercado Público and Hercílio Luz bridge viewpoints
Lagoa da Conceição kayak or stand-up paddle rental
Joaquina dunes and beach circuit
Sunset drinks around Lagoa

days.theme: Ilha do Campeche or Historic Forts

activities.name: Boat to Ilha do Campeche for beach and trails
Fortaleza de São José da Ponta Grossa
Santo Antônio de Lisboa village for late lunch
Optional oyster-heavy dinner in Ribeirão da Ilha

Paraty.name: Paraty
Paraty.note: Historic-centre pousadas let you explore on foot, but the stone streets flood after very heavy rain, so keep shoes with grip and avoid wheeled luggage inside the old town.

activities.name: Stone-street walk through the UNESCO-listed historic centre
Santa Rita Church and waterfront photo stops
Cachaçaria Maria Izabel or nearby distillery tasting
Casa da Cultura de Paraty and artisan shops
Shared schooner cruise through Paraty Bay islands and beaches
Snorkel mask rental for swim stops
Jabaquara beach or kayak add-on after the boat returns
Relaxed final evening in the lantern-lit old town

connections.method: LATAM or GOL shuttle flight between SDU/GIG and CGH/GRU in about 1 hour, with 1001 executive bus as the rail-free budget fallback
Direct LATAM or GOL flight from Congonhas or Guarulhos to Foz do Iguaçu, about 1 hr 45 min; Catarinense overnight bus is the slower backup
Domestic flight, usually via São Paulo, on LATAM or GOL; same-day air routing is far more practical than the 30+ hour overland option
LATAM or GOL flight, usually via São Paulo, taking about 4–6 hours total including the connection
Catarinense overnight bus to São Paulo plus a Costa Verde connection onward to Paraty, or fly back to Rio/São Paulo and transfer by road

extras: Use shoulder season from May to October for the easiest multi-city weather mix; swap Florianópolis for extra Rio or Salvador beach time if you want true swim-first summer conditions.
Brazil is card-friendly in cities, but keep small cash for kiosks, beach chairs, boat operators, and old-town Paraty shops.
Book Christ the Redeemer, Sugarloaf, and Iguaçu park tickets in advance on weekends or holidays because timed slots can sell out.
For fewer days, keep Rio de Janeiro, Iguaçu Falls, Salvador, and one of either São Paulo or Paraty as the core route.
Domestic fares move quickly around Carnival and year-end, so lock long-haul flight legs before filling in buses and pousadas.
If you prefer surf or beach clubs over museums, add extra nights in Florianópolis and trim São Paulo first.

Ipanema beach Rio de Janeiro sunset
São Paulo: Beco do Batman Sao Paulo murals
Iguaçu Falls: Parque das Aves Foz do Iguacu
